title_en: Fantagraphics' Bitchy! The Exasperating Existence of Midge McCracken Collects All of Cartoonist Roberta Gregory's Acclaimed Series in One Massive Volume
summary_en: Indie comics fans will want to check out our exclusive preview of Fantagraphics' massive new graphic novel Bitchy! The Exasperating Existence of Midge McCracken.
body_en:
Cartoonist Roberta Gregory is the mind behind Naughty Bits, an award-winning comic series published by Fantagraphics between 1991 and 2004. That entire run is finally being collected in one volume, now known as Bitchy! The Exasperating Existence of Midge McCracken, and it's one that deserves a spot in your collection.
With Bitchy! The Exasperating Existence of Midge McCracken now in bookstores, IGN can exclusively debut a new preview of the book. Check it out in the slideshow gallery below, though beware of some NSFW content :
Here's Fantagraphics' official summary of the book:
"1969 was definitely a different time and place, as these stories make painfully clear," Gregory tells IGN about the above excerpt. "Midge has survived the ordeal in all its graphic detail, and most importantly of all, Mom never found out. Midge’s evolving relationship with her mother is a major component of her life story."
Bitchy! The Exasperating Existence of Midge McCracken is in stores now. You can order a copy on Amazon .
For more, learn about the big changes unfolding at Marvel Comics and see our exclusive preview of Sonic the Hedgehog x Godzilla #2 .
Jesse is a mild-mannered staff writer for IGN. Allow him to lend a machete to your intellectual thicket by following @jschedeen on BlueSky .
